tweetText: Generative AI can now recompose your photos by adjusting angles and perspectives. This technology enhances creativity and allows for more dynamic visual storytelling. via Google Research Blog.

AI is now helping businesses cut security costs by discovering vulnerabilities faster and more effectively. This is huge because it shifts the balance back in favor of companies, making it harder for attackers to exploit weaknesses. via AI News

AI is making inroads in law firms as they start to adopt it for closing summaries, moving from skepticism to trial use. This shift could enhance efficiency and change how legal work is approached. via AI News

AI is transforming forex trading with advanced bots that analyze huge data sets and spot patterns faster than humans. This shift to automation could reshape how traders operate in the global currency markets. via AI News.

OpenAI is now offering ChatGPT for Clinicians free to verified U.S. healthcare professionals. This move enhances support for patient care and streamlines documentation and research in the medical field. via OpenAI News
ChatGPT now features workspace agents that automate complex tasks and connect securely across tools. This will help teams work more efficiently and scale their operations. via OpenAI News
ChatGPT now allows users to build and scale workspace agents to automate tasks and connect tools seamlessly. This will enhance team operations and boost productivity. via OpenAI News

India's app market is thriving, especially in streaming and AI, but global platforms are reaping most of the profits. This highlights the challenges local developers face in competing on a global scale. via TechCrunch AI
Google just upgraded Workspace with new AI features that act like your office intern. This makes tasks easier and boosts productivity for users. via TechCrunch AI

X is launching AI-powered custom feeds to replace Communities, featuring Grok-curated content and new ad slots. This shift could change how users engage and discover content on the platform. via TechCrunch AI
